channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: UNICEF USA Paid Internships 2026: Open Roles, Salary, Eligibility, Benefits and How to Apply
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
If you are looking for a paid internship in the nonprofit and international development sector, the UNICEF USA Paid Internship Program is one of the most competitive and rewarding opportunities available in the United States. This article provides a comprehensive, SEO-optimized guide covering currently open internship roles, salary, eligibility requirements, benefits, and the full application […]

The post UNICEF USA Paid Internships 2026: Open Roles, Salary, Eligibility, Benefits and How to Apply appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
If you are looking for a paid internship in the nonprofit and international development sector, the UNICEF USA Paid Internship Program is one of the most competitive and rewarding opportunities available in the United States.

This article provides a comprehensive, SEO-optimized guide covering currently open internship roles, salary, eligibility requirements, benefits, and the full application process.

About UNICEF USA Internships

UNICEF USA offers a limited number of paid internships each year for students interested in advancing their careers in global development, humanitarian work, policy, communications, and fundraising.

Interns gain exposure to UNICEF’s mission, develop practical skills, and contribute to initiatives that support children worldwide.

Internship Salary and Duration

  • Hourly Pay: $18.00 per hour
  • Duration: 12 weeks (Summer internships)
  • Working Hours: Up to 30 hours per week
  • Recruitment Cycle: Once annually (Summer intake)

These internships are fully paid, making them highly attractive for students seeking meaningful and compensated work experience.

Internship Location and Work Setup

  • Internships are offered in remote or hybrid formats
  • Candidates must be physically based in the United States throughout the internship
  • Interns must have access to a reliable internet connection and a computer

UNICEF USA does not provide relocation, housing, or travel support.

Eligibility Criteria

To qualify for a UNICEF USA internship, applicants must:

  • Be at least 18 years old
  • Be currently enrolled in a degree-granting academic program
  • Have valid authorization to work in the United States
  • Be able to complete the internship within the U.S.

Important notes:

  • Visa sponsorship is not provided
  • International internships are not available under UNICEF USA

Benefits of UNICEF USA Internships

Interns receive a range of professional and developmental benefits, including:

  • Paid work experience in the nonprofit and international development sector
  • Access to a dedicated manager or mentor
  • Opportunities to network with UNICEF professionals
  • Participation in intern-specific workshops and organization-wide events
  • Access to the Gallup CliftonStrengths® tool for personal development
  • Potential eligibility for academic course credit

The Internship Experience

Interns at UNICEF USA are integrated into a structured and supportive environment. Key aspects of the experience include:

  • Joining a cohort of fellow interns
  • Participating in learning sessions and internal events
  • Working on real projects aligned with UNICEF’s goals
  • Building transferable skills in research, communications, advocacy, and program support

Current Open UNICEF USA Internships (2026)

Below is a list of currently open internship opportunities:

  • Integrated Marketing and Events Intern
  • Marketing Project Management Intern
  • Digital Marketing and E-Commerce Intern
  • Community Alliance for Child Rights Intern
  • Foundation Partnerships Intern
  • Corporate and Civil Society Partnerships Intern
  • Major Giving Intern
  • Brand Marketing and Communications Intern
  • Global Policy and Public Affairs Intern
  • UNICEF Data and Insights Research Intern
  • UNICEF Next Generation Intern

These roles span multiple functional areas including marketing, partnerships, policy, research, and communications.

Other Open Roles at UNICEF USA

In addition to internships, UNICEF USA currently has several full-time opportunities available:

  • Assistant Director, Philanthropy Reporting and Analytics
  • Community Engagement Associate (multiple locations)
  • Manager, Philanthropy Reporting and Analytics
  • Vice President, Corporate and Civil Society Partnerships
  • Senior Project Manager, Integrated Campaigns

These roles may be relevant for early-career and experienced professionals seeking long-term opportunities.

How to Apply for UNICEF USA Internships

The application process typically follows these steps:

  1. Submit an online application through the UNICEF USA careers portal
  2. Participate in a phone screening
  3. Attend virtual interviews via Zoom
  4. Receive a verbal offer if selected
  5. Receive a formal written offer

Applicants are encouraged to apply early, as positions are limited and highly competitive.

Equity, Inclusion and Organizational Values

UNICEF USA is committed to fostering a workplace grounded in:

  • Children and their Rights
  • Big Thinking and Bold Action
  • The Power of collective collaboration

The organization prioritizes equity, inclusion, and belonging, ensuring that diverse perspectives are reflected in its workforce and programming.

Important Considerations

Applicants should be aware that:

  • Interns are responsible for their own travel, accommodation, and living expenses
  • Internship opportunities are limited and competitive
  • Recruitment occurs once per year, typically for summer positions

Why Apply for UNICEF USA Internships

A UNICEF USA internship offers a strong foundation for careers in:

  • International development
  • Public policy and advocacy
  • Nonprofit management
  • Communications and marketing
  • Research and data analysis

Working with UNICEF USA provides valuable exposure to one of the most recognized organizations focused on improving the lives of children globally.

Submit an online application through the UNICEF USA careers portal

Conclusion

The UNICEF USA Paid Internship Program 2026 is a highly valuable opportunity for students seeking paid, impactful experience in the nonprofit and international development sector. With competitive compensation, structured mentorship, and meaningful work, it stands out as one of the top internship programs in the United States.

For more internships with UNICEF outside the USA, please CLICK HERE https://opportunitiesforyouth.org/2026/03/15/unicef-internship-programme-2021-fully-funded-internships/

The post UNICEF USA Paid Internships 2026: Open Roles, Salary, Eligibility, Benefits and How to Apply appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Join CLPR in 2026: Exciting Legal & Policy Career Opportunities in India (Senior Research, Research & Litigation Roles)
item.dc:creator: Hadir Arfaoui
item.description:
The Centre for Law and Policy Research (CLPR) is inviting applications for multiple full-time positions in 2026, offering a unique opportunity for legal and public policy professionals to work at the intersection of constitutional law, social justice, and impact-driven research. CLPR is a not-for-profit organization committed to making the Constitution accessible and effective for all. […]

The post Join CLPR in 2026: Exciting Legal & Policy Career Opportunities in India (Senior Research, Research & Litigation Roles) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Centre for Law and Policy Research (CLPR) is inviting applications for multiple full-time positions in 2026, offering a unique opportunity for legal and public policy professionals to work at the intersection of constitutional law, social justice, and impact-driven research.

CLPR is a not-for-profit organization committed to making the Constitution accessible and effective for all. Through legal research, strategic litigation, and policy advocacy, the organization addresses systemic discrimination affecting marginalized communities, including those impacted by caste, gender, disability, and minority status.

With offices in Bengaluru and New Delhi, CLPR collaborates with academics, civil society organizations, activists, and policymakers to drive meaningful change through law and governance.

Why Work at CLPR?

Working at CLPR means contributing to impactful, rights-based initiatives while developing expertise in legal research, litigation, and public policy. Employees are part of a collaborative environment that values inclusion, diversity, and social transformation.

Key benefits include:

  • Direct involvement in constitutional and social justice work
  • Opportunities to engage in high-impact litigation and policy reform
  • Exposure to interdisciplinary collaboration with experts and institutions
  • Professional growth in research, advocacy, and public engagement
  • Inclusive workplace encouraging diversity and equal opportunity

Available Positions

1. Senior Research Associate (Full-Time)

Location: Bengaluru or New Delhi

This senior-level role involves leading legal research, supporting litigation strategies, and contributing to advocacy and outreach efforts.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Conduct doctrinal, comparative, and empirical legal research
  • Draft reports, policy briefs, and academic publications
  • Support litigation and advocacy initiatives
  • Coordinate with stakeholders and manage projects
  • Contribute to communications and public engagement

Requirements:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Law (mandatory)
  • Postgraduate degree (preferred)
  • Minimum 4 years of relevant experience
  • Strong research, writing, and analytical skills
  • Demonstrated commitment to social justice

APPLY HERE

2. Research Associate (Full-Time)

Location: Bengaluru or New Delhi

An entry to mid-level role ideal for early-career professionals interested in legal research and policy advocacy.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Analyze legal materials and policy documents
  • Conduct research on constitutional and social justice issues
  • Draft publications and support outreach
  • Assist in project management and stakeholder coordination

Requirements:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Law
  • 0–3 years of experience
  • Strong writing, research, and organizational skills
  • Interest in rights-based work

APPLY HERE 

3. Litigation Associate (Full-Time)

Location: Bengaluru

This role focuses on strategic litigation and legal aid, particularly in constitutional rights cases.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Draft petitions, affidavits, and legal documents
  • Represent clients in courts, including High Courts and the Supreme Court
  • Conduct legal research for ongoing cases
  • Collaborate with senior advocates

Requirements:

  • Law degree and Bar Council enrollment
  • Minimum 2 years of litigation experience
  • Fluency in Kannada
  • Strong courtroom, drafting, and analytical skills

APPLY HERE 

Equal Opportunity Commitment

CLPR is an equal opportunity employer. Applications are strongly encouraged from individuals belonging to marginalized and underrepresented communities, including SC/ST groups, minority religions, LGBTQIA+ individuals, and persons with disabilities.

Deadline

No specific deadline has been provided. Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, so early submission is highly recommended.

Conclusion

If you are passionate about constitutional law, social justice, and policy reform, CLPR offers a meaningful career path where your work directly contributes to societal impact. Whether you are an experienced legal researcher or an early-career professional, these roles provide a platform to grow while making a difference.

For more information click here 

Discover more opportunities here 

The post Join CLPR in 2026: Exciting Legal & Policy Career Opportunities in India (Senior Research, Research & Litigation Roles) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Lee Kong Chian Research Fellowship 2026 in Singapore: Fully Funded Six-Month Opportunity for Researchers in Southeast Asian Studies
item.dc:creator: Hadir Arfaoui
item.description:
The Lee Kong Chian Research Fellowship 2026 is one of the most prestigious research opportunities in Asia, offering scholars, historians, and advanced researchers the chance to immerse themselves in world-class archival collections in Singapore. Hosted by the National Library Board Singapore, this fully funded six-month residential fellowship provides unparalleled access to rare historical materials and […]

The post Lee Kong Chian Research Fellowship 2026 in Singapore: Fully Funded Six-Month Opportunity for Researchers in Southeast Asian Studies appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Lee Kong Chian Research Fellowship 2026 is one of the most prestigious research opportunities in Asia, offering scholars, historians, and advanced researchers the chance to immerse themselves in world-class archival collections in Singapore. Hosted by the National Library Board Singapore, this fully funded six-month residential fellowship provides unparalleled access to rare historical materials and contemporary archives related to Singapore and Southeast Asia.

With a strong emphasis on academic excellence and impactful research, this fellowship is designed for individuals seeking to deepen their expertise while contributing to global scholarship in history, culture, and regional studies.

About the Lee Kong Chian Research Fellowship

The Lee Kong Chian Research Fellowship is a six-month residential programme based in the heart of Singapore. Fellows are required to conduct research using the extensive collections housed at the Lee Kong Chian Reference Library and the National Archives of Singapore.

These collections are among the richest in Asia and include:

  • Historical materials dating back to the 16th century
  • Early European accounts of Southeast Asia
  • Rare maps and navigational charts of the region
  • Publications from Singapore’s earliest printing presses
  • Archives of prominent Singaporean authors
  • Government and institutional records of historical significance

The fellowship enables researchers to explore both historical and contemporary narratives, making it ideal for interdisciplinary and comparative research projects.

Fellowship Deadline

The application deadline for the Lee Kong Chian Research Fellowship 2026 is:

29 May 2026

Applicants are strongly encouraged to prepare their research proposals and supporting documents well in advance to ensure a competitive submission.

Financial Benefits and Support

The Lee Kong Chian Research Fellowship offers a comprehensive financial package to support fellows throughout their residency:

  • Monthly stipend for the full six-month duration
  • One-time relocation allowance (for international fellows)
  • Monthly accommodation allowance
  • Reimbursement of a return economy-class air ticket

This funding structure ensures that fellows can fully dedicate their time to research without financial constraints.

Research Focus and Areas

The fellowship is open to a wide range of research topics, particularly those that engage with:

  • Singapore’s historical development
  • Southeast Asian regional studies
  • Colonial and postcolonial narratives
  • Cultural heritage and archival studies
  • Political, economic, and social transformations in the region

Researchers are encouraged to design projects that make meaningful use of the library and archival collections.

Eligibility Criteria

The programme welcomes applications from:

  • Curators
  • Historians
  • Academics
  • Independent researchers
  • Doctoral and postdoctoral scholars
  • Individuals conducting advanced-level research

Applicants must demonstrate a strong track record of achievement in their respective fields and present a well-defined research proposal aligned with the available collections.

Fellowship Expectations and Obligations

Recipients of the fellowship are expected to:

  • Reside in Singapore for the full six-month duration
  • Conduct research primarily using the Lee Kong Chian Reference Library collections
  • Actively engage with the academic and research community
  • Produce scholarly outputs based on their research

The residency requirement ensures that fellows fully benefit from direct access to the archives and research environment.

Publishing Rights and Academic Contribution

Fellows retain the opportunity to publish their research findings, contributing to academic discourse and enhancing global understanding of Southeast Asian history and development.

The fellowship also encourages dissemination through:

  • Academic publications
  • Conferences and seminars
  • Collaborative research initiatives

Selection Criteria

Applications are evaluated based on:

  • Academic and professional achievements
  • Quality and originality of the research proposal
  • Relevance to the available collections
  • Potential contribution to scholarship and knowledge production

The selection process is highly competitive, attracting candidates from across the globe.

How to Apply

Interested candidates must submit a complete application before the deadline, including:

  • A detailed research proposal
  • Academic CV
  • Supporting documents demonstrating research experience
  • Any additional required materials specified by the programme

Applicants should ensure that their proposal clearly demonstrates how they intend to utilize the archival collections.

Voices from Previous Fellows

The National Library Board Singapore has previously awarded the fellowship to outstanding researchers, including:

  • Nurul Kaiyisah Mohd Latip
  • Muneerah Ab Razak
  • Sophia Chen Zhiting
  • Chng Xin Ying
  • Jennifer Yang
  • Han Song

These fellows have highlighted the programme’s strong academic environment, rich archival access, and transformative research opportunities.

Why You Should Apply

The Lee Kong Chian Research Fellowship stands out as a unique opportunity to:

  • Access rare and valuable historical collections
  • Conduct high-impact research in a global hub
  • Engage with leading scholars and institutions
  • Advance your academic or professional career

For researchers passionate about Southeast Asia, history, and archival work, this fellowship offers an unmatched platform for intellectual growth and contribution.

For more information click here

Discover more opportunities here 

The post Lee Kong Chian Research Fellowship 2026 in Singapore: Fully Funded Six-Month Opportunity for Researchers in Southeast Asian Studies appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: 2026 Cook Center Summer Research Fellowships for Duke University Students
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
The Samuel DuBois Cook Center on Social Equity at Duke University is now accepting applications for its 2026 Summer Research Fellowships. These fellowships are open to both graduate and undergraduate students and are designed to provide hands-on experience in cutting-edge research focused on social inequality. Participants gain the opportunity to deepen their scholarship, expand their […]

The post 2026 Cook Center Summer Research Fellowships for Duke University Students appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Samuel DuBois Cook Center on Social Equity at Duke University is now accepting applications for its 2026 Summer Research Fellowships. These fellowships are open to both graduate and undergraduate students and are designed to provide hands-on experience in cutting-edge research focused on social inequality. Participants gain the opportunity to deepen their scholarship, expand their professional networks, and actively contribute to the Cook Center’s mission of advancing social equity.

The Cook Center conducts interdisciplinary research aimed at understanding the structures and systems that drive inequality in society. Its research pillars include educational and economic opportunity, health equity, carceral justice, and public policy engagement. The Center emphasizes a data-driven approach and methodological innovation, providing students with exposure to advanced research techniques and analytical tools.

Fellows are integrated into dynamic research teams where they collaborate with faculty, postdoctoral researchers, graduate and undergraduate students, and other research professionals. Graduate fellows may mentor undergraduate students while simultaneously receiving guidance from senior scholars, ensuring a layered mentorship environment. This collaborative structure promotes leadership development, teamwork, and a comprehensive understanding of research practice in social equity.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Inequality remains a pressing challenge worldwide. The Cook Center Summer Research Fellowships provide students with the opportunity to investigate the causes and consequences of inequality and to develop evidence-based solutions. Through participation in this program, students gain valuable insight into how research informs policy, social programs, and public debate.

The program emphasizes mentorship, collaborative research, and professional development, equipping students with the skills needed to succeed in academia, policy, and advocacy. Fellows gain practical experience, broaden their networks, and enhance their understanding of interdisciplinary approaches to social equity research. These experiences are crucial for students pursuing careers in research, public policy, education, and other fields focused on social justice.

By fostering a diverse community of scholars, the Cook Center ensures that participants are prepared to contribute meaningfully to efforts aimed at reducing inequality and promoting social equity both nationally and globally.

What You’ll Gain

The Cook Center Summer Research Fellowships offer a wide range of professional, academic, and personal benefits:

  • Stipend: Graduate fellows receive $10,625.01 for the summer term, while undergraduate fellows receive $2,500 per term, with the possibility of earning $5,000 if participating in both terms.
  • Mentorship: Individualized guidance from faculty, postdoctoral researchers, and senior scholars provides valuable career support.
  • Research Experience: Fellows gain practical skills by contributing to ongoing research projects, including data collection, literature review, analysis, and research dissemination.
  • Skill Development: Build expertise in research methods, statistical analysis, interdisciplinary collaboration, project management, and communication.
  • Networking: Engage with peers, mentors, and professionals across multiple disciplines, creating opportunities for future collaboration.
  • Professional Exposure: Fellows share research outcomes through the Cook Center website and may gain visibility in academic and professional communities.

Graduate fellows will also have the opportunity to mentor undergraduate fellows, fostering leadership and teaching skills, while undergraduates gain exposure to advanced research practices and interdisciplinary approaches to studying inequality.

Eligibility

Graduate Fellows:

  • Must be current Duke University PhD students in good standing, enrolled for the Fall 2026 semester.
  • Must coordinate with their department to ensure summer funding arrangements are compatible.

Undergraduate Fellows:

  • Must be current Duke University student enrolled for Fall 2026.
  • Must be eligible to work in the United States.

All Fellows:

  • Must have a strong interest in social equity research and commit to active participation in fellowship activities.
  • Must be able to attend biweekly team meetings and contribute to research projects for the duration of the fellowship.

Application Process and Deadline

Applicants interested in the 2026 Cook Center Summer Research Fellowships must download the appropriate application form for graduate or undergraduate students from the Cook Center website. All required materials, including completed application forms, curriculum vitae, statements of research interests, and letters of recommendation, should be compiled into a single PDF. Applicants will then submit their completed application according to the instructions provided on the official website.

Deadlines:

  • Priority consideration: March 31, 2026
  • Rolling applications: April 30, 2026, depending on availability

To learn more and submit your application, visit the official Cook Center website.

Explore More Opportunities for Youth

Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities.

The post 2026 Cook Center Summer Research Fellowships for Duke University Students appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: MSc in Olympic Studies 2026–2027: Advance Your Career in Olympic Education
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
The University of Peloponnese, in partnership with the International Olympic Academy (IOA), invites applications for the Master’s Degree Programme in “Olympic Studies, Olympic Education, Organization and Management of Olympic Events” (MSc) for the academic year 2026–2027. This internationally recognized programme is supported by Olympic Solidarity, an initiative of the International Olympic Committee (IOC), which offers […]

The post MSc in Olympic Studies 2026–2027: Advance Your Career in Olympic Education appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The University of Peloponnese, in partnership with the International Olympic Academy (IOA), invites applications for the Master’s Degree Programme in “Olympic Studies, Olympic Education, Organization and Management of Olympic Events” (MSc) for the academic year 2026–2027. This internationally recognized programme is supported by Olympic Solidarity, an initiative of the International Olympic Committee (IOC), which offers scholarships for selected candidates.

The programme is designed for graduates and professionals who want to deepen their knowledge of the Olympic Movement, gain expertise in Olympic philosophy, education, and sport management, and contribute to the global development of sport and Olympic education. Classes are conducted in Ancient Olympia and Sparta, Greece, providing students with a unique immersive experience at the historical heart of Olympism.

Through a combination of on-site lectures, practical workshops, and a master’s thesis completed remotely, the MSc offers an interdisciplinary approach, integrating education, sport, culture, and management. Students also benefit from the opportunity to join a global network of professionals and organizations involved in the Olympic Movement.

What You’ll Gain

Participants in the MSc will gain multiple benefits that advance their academic and professional careers:

  • Specialized Knowledge: Acquire advanced understanding of Olympic philosophy, pedagogy, and the historical and modern Olympic Games.
  • Practical Experience: Learn the organization and management of Olympic events and major sports competitions.
  • Professional Development: Gain skills in sports marketing, sponsorship, strategic planning, and financial management of sports organizations.
  • Global Networking: Connect with Olympic committees, international federations, and professionals engaged in sports education, culture, and event management.
  • Scholarship Opportunities: Tuition and full-boarding scholarships are available through National Olympic Committees for eligible candidates.

By combining theory and practical application, this programme prepares graduates to contribute to Olympic education programs, lead sport organizations, and participate actively in the international Olympic Movement.

Eligibility

Candidates must meet the following criteria:

  • Hold a bachelor’s degree from a recognized Greek or foreign higher education institution. Preference is given to officials of National Olympic Academies, National Olympic Committees, the International Olympic Committee, international and national federations, and researchers in Olympic studies or sport management.
  • Graduates from related fields such as education, sport, management, humanities, journalism, economics, pedagogy, or physical education are also eligible.
  • Candidates must demonstrate professional experience, participation in sport events, Olympic education activities, or related research.
  • Proficiency in English is required, with certificates for other languages considered an asset.

Applicants must provide supporting documentation, including transcripts, CV, recommendation letters, identification, proof of participation in relevant activities, and any certifications relevant to their experience. Shortlisted candidates will be interviewed online.

Programme Structure

The MSc programme runs for three semesters:

  • First Semester (18 Sept – 21 Nov 2026): On-site lectures in Ancient Olympia and Sparta, covering the history and philosophy of sport, modern Olympic Games, Olympic pedagogy, ethics, and research methods.
  • Second Semester (3 Apr – 6 Jun 2027): On-site lectures in Greece, with modules on Olympic pedagogy, organization of games and major sports events, sports marketing, and optional modules such as integrity in sport, financial management, and sport policies.
  • Third Semester (Sept 2027 – Feb 2028): Completion and submission of the master’s thesis, conducted remotely.

All lectures are taught in English, and students are required to attend lectures, submit assignments, and complete examinations to progress successfully.

Costs and Scholarships

Tuition fees for the programme are 5,000 € for the three semesters, payable in three installments. Full-boarding at the IOA premises is approximately 3,500 €, with final costs confirmed individually.

Scholarships are available through National Olympic Committees via IOC Olympic Solidarity. Applicants are encouraged to declare interest in scholarships in their application form and contact their National Olympic Committees for further guidance.

Application Process and Deadline

To apply, candidates must submit the following certified documents both via email and in hard copy to the programme secretariat by 30 April 2026:

  • Completed application form
  • Bachelor’s degree certificate and transcript, including thesis title and grade
  • CV detailing studies, professional experience, research, and extracurricular activities
  • Language proficiency certificates
  • Passport or ID copy
  • Recommendation letters from professors or relevant sport organizations
  • Supporting documents for professional experience, participation in sports, or Olympic activities

👉 To learn more and apply, visit the official MSc in Olympic Studies programme page.

Explore More Opportunities for Youth

Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities.

The post MSc in Olympic Studies 2026–2027: Advance Your Career in Olympic Education appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: World Literacy Foundation Youth Ambassador Program 2026: Empowering Young Leaders for Global Literacy
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
About the Opportunity The World Literacy Foundation (WLF) Youth Ambassador Program 2026 is a global initiative designed to empower young people to become active leaders in the fight against illiteracy. Through this program, participants aged 16 to 25 will join a worldwide community of changemakers, connect with peers across more than 60 countries, and develop […]

The post World Literacy Foundation Youth Ambassador Program 2026: Empowering Young Leaders for Global Literacy appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
About the Opportunity

The World Literacy Foundation (WLF) Youth Ambassador Program 2026 is a global initiative designed to empower young people to become active leaders in the fight against illiteracy. Through this program, participants aged 16 to 25 will join a worldwide community of changemakers, connect with peers across more than 60 countries, and develop skills to advocate for literacy at local and global levels.

WLF is an international non-profit organization dedicated to reducing illiteracy and promoting equitable access to education. Its Youth Ambassador Program is grounded in the principle of youth empowerment, believing that young people’s creativity, vision, and energy are key to achieving meaningful impact. Over a three-month period from June to September 2026, 1000 young leaders will participate in online modules, collaborative projects, and advocacy initiatives designed to give them the tools to make a measurable difference in their communities.

The program is entirely online, allowing participants from around the world to join without geographic constraints. Training focuses on leadership, advocacy, fundraising, social media communication, and project implementation. Ambassadors will gain hands-on experience designing campaigns, engaging with communities, and amplifying literacy initiatives. Participants who fundraise for WLF will receive a formal letter of completion, while all participants will receive a certificate recognizing their efforts upon completion of the program.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Literacy is a foundational skill for education, empowerment, and equitable development. Around the world, millions of children face barriers to learning due to poverty, conflict, or lack of resources. The Youth Ambassador Program enables young people to address these systemic challenges by raising awareness, mobilizing resources, and implementing advocacy campaigns.

By participating, ambassadors gain a unique perspective on global literacy issues and learn how to create change that is both local and international. The program not only amplifies youth voices but also cultivates the next generation of global leaders committed to equitable education. In addition, ambassadors contribute to innovative solutions to combat illiteracy, ensuring that knowledge and learning opportunities reach underserved populations.

What You’ll Gain

The Youth Ambassador Program provides participants with multiple benefits that extend beyond the immediate three-month program:

  • Skill Development: Ambassadors will build leadership, advocacy, communication, public relations, and fundraising skills applicable in both educational and professional contexts.
  • Global Network: Participants join a diverse cohort of 1000 young leaders, fostering international collaboration and shared learning.
  • Real-World Impact: By leading literacy campaigns and fundraising initiatives, ambassadors contribute directly to increasing access to education for vulnerable children globally.
  • Recognition and Visibility: Ambassadors will have opportunities to have their voices amplified through WLF’s website, social media channels, and mainstream media features.
  • Practical Experience in Advocacy: Through youth-led campaigns and creative projects, participants gain first-hand experience in social impact initiatives and community mobilization.

This combination of benefits ensures that participants are not only contributing to literacy efforts but are also growing as capable, confident global leaders.

Eligibility

To participate in the Youth Ambassador Program 2026, applicants must meet the following criteria:

  • Be 16 to 25 years old at the time of the program.
  • Demonstrate a strong interest in literacy, education, human rights, or social impact.
  • Be motivated to join a global community of young changemakers.
  • Have access to a computer or smartphone and a reliable internet connection for online activities.
  • Be active on social media and willing to use their voice to advocate for literacy.
  • Commit to approximately 30 hours over three months to complete ambassador tasks.
  • Be willing to support fundraising initiatives for WLF to expand global literacy programs.

This program is inclusive, welcoming applicants from all countries and backgrounds who are passionate about literacy and social change.

Application Process and Deadline

Applicants begin by visiting the official World Literacy Foundation Ambassador Program page and selecting “Apply Now.” The website provides step-by-step instructions for completing the application form and submitting all required information. Applicants should carefully review the Terms and Conditions before applying, as the program is recurring annually and requires active commitment to the tasks outlined.

The program will run from June to September 2026, and applications are reviewed on a rolling basis. Early submission is encouraged to secure a spot in the global cohort.

To learn more and apply, visit the official World Literacy Foundation website.

Explore More Opportunities for Youth

Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities.

The post World Literacy Foundation Youth Ambassador Program 2026: Empowering Young Leaders for Global Literacy appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Fully Funded PhD Scholarships at UQ: Co-Develop Solutions to Climate-Driven Loss and Damage in the Pacific
item.dc:creator: Maria Ramirez
item.description:
The University of Queensland (UQ) is offering four fully funded PhD scholarships as part of the ARC Industry Laureate Fellowship, focused on co-developing solutions to climate-driven loss and damage in the Pacific. This is a unique opportunity to work closely with communities, governments, and regional partners to address the social, cultural, and justice dimensions of […]

The post Fully Funded PhD Scholarships at UQ: Co-Develop Solutions to Climate-Driven Loss and Damage in the Pacific appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The University of Queensland (UQ) is offering four fully funded PhD scholarships as part of the ARC Industry Laureate Fellowship, focused on co-developing solutions to climate-driven loss and damage in the Pacific. This is a unique opportunity to work closely with communities, governments, and regional partners to address the social, cultural, and justice dimensions of climate change.

These scholarships are ideal for candidates passionate about applied research and committed to creating meaningful change for Pacific Island communities.

Why Apply?

PhD candidates will engage in research that directly impacts communities in the Cook Islands, Fiji, and Vanuatu, offering a rare combination of academic, field, and policy experience. Key benefits include:

  • Fully funded scholarship covering tuition fees, living stipend (AU$37,500 per annum, tax-free), and Overseas Student Health Cover
  • Access to world-class facilities and a dynamic, interdisciplinary research environment at the School of the Environment
  • Funded fieldwork and collaboration with NGOs, regional organizations, and government agencies
  • Mentorship from leading experts, including Professor Karen McNamara, Principal Supervisor

Research Focus

PhD projects will be co-designed with your advisory team to align with the Fellowship’s objectives:

  1. Develop and adapt methods for assessing climate-driven loss and damage
  2. Document lived experiences and community-led responses
  3. Implement and evaluate locally grounded interventions
  4. Inform policy and practice through multi-scalar insights and best-practice guidelines

Projects are flexible and tailored to your research interests, disciplinary background, and the needs of partner communities.

Who Should Apply

Ideal candidates will have academic achievement in:

  • Geography
  • Environmental science
  • Indigenous studies
  • Development studies or related fields

Experience or interest in climate adaptation, participatory research, or policy engagement is highly desirable. Scholars from Pacific Island nations, particularly Cook Islands, Fiji, and Vanuatu, are encouraged to apply.

How to Submit an Expression of Interest (EOI)

Submitting an EOI is the first step toward securing your PhD scholarship. Here’s what you need to know:

Step 1: Prepare Your EOI

Include the following details:

  • Proposed supervisor
  • Scholarship details
  • Previous academic qualifications
  • Referee contacts (minimum two)
  • English language proficiency information

Step 2: Upload Required Documents

  • Academic curriculum vitae (CV)
  • Academic transcripts and degree certificates
  • English language documents (if applicable)

Formatting Guidelines:

  • PDF format only
  • File name: LASTNAME_firstname_document-name.pdf
  • Include both original and official English translations if documents are in another language

Step 3: Academic CV

Your CV should focus on academic and research achievements, including:

  • Personal details (name, contact, nationality, languages, ORCID ID)
  • Education and academic awards
  • Employment history and research experience
  • Research outputs, publications, and grants
  • Optional: a 200-word contextual statement on achievements relative to opportunities

Do not include photographs, hobbies, or unrelated personal details.

Step 4: Referee Details

Provide at least two referees who can speak to your academic or research abilities. UQ will contact referees directly; do not submit pre-written letters.

After Submitting Your EOI

  • Your nominated supervisor will review your EOI (response typically within two weeks).
  • If the supervisor is interested, an informal interview may be arranged to discuss your research fit.
  • If supported, you will be invited to submit a final application, including:
    • Passport copy
    • Additional documents (proof of citizenship, name change, previous research studies)
    • UQ-approved agent submission for some international applicants

If your EOI is not supported, you may choose another supervisor and submit a new EOI.

Deadline

All EOIs must be submitted by 15 April, 2026. Early submission is strongly recommended.

How to Apply

To start your application, visit the official UQ project page: UQ PhD Projects – Climate-Driven Loss and Damage

APPLY HERE. 

For more information about this apportunity, click here. 

Discover more scholarship opportunities, click here. 

Deadline: 15 April, 2026

For questions about the suitability of the project, contact Professor Karen McNamara at karen.mcnamara@uq.edu.au. Submit your expression of interest (EOI) via UQ PhD Projects.

The post Fully Funded PhD Scholarships at UQ: Co-Develop Solutions to Climate-Driven Loss and Damage in the Pacific appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: EU Call for Proposals in Ethiopia 2026: Funding for Civil Society and Global Gateway Action
item.dc:creator: Maria Ramirez
item.description:
A Major EU Funding Opportunity for Civil Society Organisations in Ethiopia The Delegation of the European Union to Ethiopia has opened a significant new funding opportunity for civil society organisations through the call titled “Supporting CSO role in sustainable and inclusive development and in Global Gateway Investments.” This initiative is designed to strengthen Ethiopian civil […]

The post EU Call for Proposals in Ethiopia 2026: Funding for Civil Society and Global Gateway Action appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
A Major EU Funding Opportunity for Civil Society Organisations in Ethiopia

The Delegation of the European Union to Ethiopia has opened a significant new funding opportunity for civil society organisations through the call titled “Supporting CSO role in sustainable and inclusive development and in Global Gateway Investments.” This initiative is designed to strengthen Ethiopian civil society organisations as independent, resilient, and accountable actors that can contribute to inclusive governance, equitable socio-economic development, and the implementation of Global Gateway investments in the country.

This call is part of the CSOs Thematic Programme 2021–2027 and builds on earlier EU support to civil society in Ethiopia. The programme reflects the EU’s broader commitment to democratic participation, rights-based development, and stronger civic engagement, while also linking civil society action to priority investment sectors under the Global Gateway strategy.

What This EU Call for Proposals Supports

The overall objective of the call is to strengthen Ethiopian civil society organisations so they can play a stronger role in inclusive governance and sustainable development, while also contributing to Global Gateway investments. The call is divided into two separate lots, and applicants may apply under one lot only. For either lot, proposals are expected to use an integrated approach and address at least three priority areas under the selected lot rather than presenting isolated activities.

This means applicants need to design proposals that are coherent, strategic, and clearly linked to measurable results. The guidelines also stress the importance of strong intervention logic, relevant indicators, monitoring plans, and meaningful partnerships between consortium members.

Funding Available: Budget, Lots, and Grant Size

The total indicative budget available under this call is EUR 5,829,000. The funding is split equally across the two lots, with EUR 2,914,500 allocated to Lot 1 and EUR 2,914,500 allocated to Lot 2. The guidelines note that the Lot 2 allocation is tied to the EU budget for 2027 and is therefore subject to the prior availability of those funds.

For both lots, the grant size is fixed at EUR 2,914,500, meaning this is a high-value funding opportunity aimed at large-scale actions. The EU contribution must represent 95% of the total eligible costs of the action, with the remaining balance financed from other sources unless the applicant can justify full financing as essential for implementation.

Lot 1: Strengthening Civil Society Capacity and Civic Space

Lot 1 focuses on strengthening civil society’s role, capacity, and enabling environment in Ethiopia. Its specific objective is to improve the capacity, sustainability, and influence of local and grassroots CSOs so they can engage more effectively in policy-making, advocacy, dialogue with authorities, monitoring, and accountability processes at both local and national levels.

Under this lot, priority areas include improving CSOs’ ability to influence public policy, support evidence-based advocacy, promote transparency and accountability, and build stronger partnerships and coalitions. It also supports inclusive social and economic development initiatives, stronger community engagement, and better internal governance, sustainability, fundraising, and organisational effectiveness. Another major focus is the protection of civic space and the promotion of an enabling legal and policy environment for civil society.

The call also places strong emphasis on Financial Support to Third Parties (FSTP) under Lot 1. Applicants are expected to establish and manage at least one open and competitive support mechanism for local and grassroots CSOs, including capacity building, mentoring, and tailored technical support. At least EUR 1,860,000 must be reserved for FSTP to local and grassroots CSOs, and at least EUR 291,000 must be allocated to strengthening the capacities of those recipients.

Lot 2: Civil Society Engagement in Global Gateway Priority Sectors

Lot 2 is aimed at strengthening civil society engagement in Global Gateway priority sectors in Ethiopia. Its objective is to enhance the role and capacity of CSOs to contribute to transparent, inclusive, and sustainable policy-making, reform processes, and investment processes in four sectors: clean energy transition, sustainable agri-food value chains, health systems strengthening, and human-centric digital transformation.

Priority areas under Lot 2 include improving governance and regulatory frameworks, increasing transparency and accountability in investment processes, empowering communities to participate in decision-making, and supporting inclusive skills development and economic opportunities linked to investment sectors. The call encourages actions that strengthen public oversight, promote environmental and social safeguards, support grievance mechanisms, and expand the role of communities and local CSOs across the investment cycle.

Actions under Lot 2 must be implemented in at least three specific locations in Ethiopia where Global Gateway projects are already being implemented, and applicants must clearly explain how their chosen areas connect to relevant Global Gateway investments.

Who Is Eligible to Apply?

This opportunity is intended for non-profit legal entities such as civil society organisations, including NGOs, networks, and associations. To qualify, the lead applicant must be a legal person, be non-profit-making, and be directly responsible for the preparation and management of the action. Eligible lead applicants must be effectively established in an EU Member State, Ethiopia, or another country eligible under Article 28 of the NDICI-Global Europe regulation.

A key requirement is that the lead applicant must apply together with at least two co-applicants established and registered in Ethiopia. In addition, the consortium must demonstrate experience managing at least one EU grant above EUR 1.5 million in the past three years, at least three years of practical experience working with local CSOs and building their capacity, and a proven track record in managing Financial Support to Third Parties schemes.

For lead applicants not established in Ethiopia, the rules are stricter. They must demonstrate prior knowledge of Ethiopia’s civil society landscape, show that they have implemented at least two projects with local CSOs, and prove that local CSOs have been involved in the conception, governance, implementation, monitoring, and reporting of the proposed action.

Project Duration, Locations, and Design Requirements

For both lots, the initial planned duration of an action must be at least 36 months and no more than 48 months.

For Lot 1, actions must take place in at least three locations from the following exclusive list: Somali, Harari, Sidama, Gambella, South West Ethiopia, and Dire Dawa City Administration.

Beyond location and duration, the guidelines require all proposals to demonstrate strong internal coherence between needs, objectives, expected results, activities, and indicators. Applicants are strongly encouraged to define one clear specific objective, include a solid monitoring plan, and describe genuine, balanced partnerships among consortium members.

The call also includes mandatory cross-cutting priorities. All proposals must integrate meaningful youth engagement, a human rights-based approach, gender equality and women’s empowerment, and conflict sensitivity throughout the design and implementation of the action.

How to Apply Through PROSPECT and PADOR

This is a restricted call for proposals, which means the first stage requires the submission of a concept note only. Applicants whose concept notes are pre-selected will later be invited to submit a full application.

Applications must be submitted online via PROSPECT, and organisations must register in PADOR. In addition, lead applicants and co-applicants must register in the European Commission’s Participant Register. The guidelines strongly recommend completing these registration steps well in advance rather than waiting until the final days before submission.

Applicants must submit their concept notes in English, follow the official application format carefully, and ensure that the concept note contains all relevant information because no extra annexes are evaluated at that first stage. The full application stage, for those invited, will require more detailed documentation, including the budget, logical framework, and supporting eligibility documents.

APPLY HERE. 

For more information about this apportunity, click here. 

Discover more grant opportunities, click here. 

Deadline and Key Dates

The deadline for submission of concept notes is 13 May 2026 at 15:30 Brussels time. This is the most important immediate deadline for interested applicants.

There will also be an information session on 17 April 2026 in hybrid format. Organisations interested in attending must send an email by 8 April 2026 with the requested participant details.

According to the indicative timetable, applicants can expect the following milestones:

  • information to pre-selected applicants and invitations to submit full applications: 25 June 2026
  • deadline for full applications: 11 August 2026 at 15:30 Brussels time
  • notification of contracting authority decision: 20 October 2026
  • indicative contract signature: 20 November 2026

Final Tips Before You Apply

For organisations considering this opportunity, early preparation will be essential. Because the call requires consortium formation, registration on multiple EU platforms, and a strong concept note aligned with one of the two lots, applicants should begin preparing as soon as possible.

It is especially important to select the correct lot from the beginning, build a proposal with a strong and coherent intervention logic, and make sure the consortium’s experience clearly matches the requirements set out in the guidelines. For organisations applying under Lot 1, the design of the FSTP component will be particularly important. For Lot 2, applicants must be precise in linking their action to actual Global Gateway project locations in Ethiopia.

For civil society organisations with the right structure, partnerships, and implementation capacity, this call represents a major opportunity to secure substantial EU support for long-term impact in Ethiopia.

The post EU Call for Proposals in Ethiopia 2026: Funding for Civil Society and Global Gateway Action appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Advance Your Academic Career with Leeds University Business School Post Doctoral Fellowships
item.dc:creator: Maria Ramirez
item.description:
Leeds University Business School (LUBS) is proud to announce its Post Doctoral Fellowship Scheme, designed to accelerate the careers of early-stage researchers. This two-year fellowship provides a structured and supportive environment, enabling scholars to build world-class research portfolios and gain valuable academic experience. The fellowships are available across multiple disciplines including Accounting & Finance, Analytics, […]

The post Advance Your Academic Career with Leeds University Business School Post Doctoral Fellowships appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Leeds University Business School (LUBS) is proud to announce its Post Doctoral Fellowship Scheme, designed to accelerate the careers of early-stage researchers. This two-year fellowship provides a structured and supportive environment, enabling scholars to build world-class research portfolios and gain valuable academic experience.

The fellowships are available across multiple disciplines including Accounting & Finance, Analytics, Technology & Operations, Economics, International Business, Management & Organisations, Marketing, and People, Work & Employment Relations. This initiative reflects LUBS’s commitment to strengthening research capacity and fostering impactful scholarship.

Fellowship Benefits

Fellows at LUBS receive:

  • Structured training programs tailored for early-career researchers
  • Dedicated mentoring on publications, funding applications, and career development
  • Opportunities for teaching experience across a variety of disciplines
  • Access to a supportive research community and cohort network

Additional benefits include excellent annual leave entitlement, generous pension schemes, family-friendly policies, fitness and wellbeing facilities, and extensive professional development opportunities.

Eligibility Requirements

  • PhD completed or near completion in a relevant field
  • Research experience through journals, conferences, or similar platforms, with strong communication skills
  • Teaching experience and ability to work independently and collaboratively
  • Strong qualitative or quantitative methodological skills with advanced research training
  • Commitment to ongoing professional development

Application Process

Applicants must engage with a potential mentor who is a current faculty member in the relevant department to discuss research plans and secure a supporting statement. This step is essential for a successful application.

Interested candidates can apply via the following links:

  • Accounting & Finance Department
  • Analytics, Technology and Operations Department
  • Economics Department
  • International Business Department
  • Marketing Department
  • Management and Organisation Department
  • People, Work and Employment Department

APPLY HERE. 

For more information about this apportunity, click here. 

Discover more fellowship opportunities, click here. 

Important Dates

Release Date: 31 March 2026
Closing Date: 31 May 2026

For international applicants, eligibility under the Skilled Worker visa or Global Talent visa may apply. Further details can be found on UK Government Skilled Worker visa and Global Talent visa pages.

 

The post Advance Your Academic Career with Leeds University Business School Post Doctoral Fellowships appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Launch Your Career in AI and Data for Global Impact: Dalberg Data Insights AI Engineering Fellowship 2026
item.dc:creator: Maria Ramirez
item.description:
Are you a recent graduate passionate about Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Data Science? Do you aspire to use your technical skills to tackle global challenges and drive social impact? The Dalberg Data Insights AI Engineering Fellowship 2026 is your gateway to hands-on experience in creating innovative data solutions for real-world development problems. About Dalberg Data […]

The post Launch Your Career in AI and Data for Global Impact: Dalberg Data Insights AI Engineering Fellowship 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Are you a recent graduate passionate about Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Data Science? Do you aspire to use your technical skills to tackle global challenges and drive social impact? The Dalberg Data Insights AI Engineering Fellowship 2026 is your gateway to hands-on experience in creating innovative data solutions for real-world development problems.

About Dalberg Data Insights

Dalberg Data Insights (DDI) bridges the AI adoption gap in Low- and Middle-Income Countries (LMICs), ensuring no population is left behind in the digital transformation. As part of the global Dalberg Group, DDI designs AI strategies, develops solutions, provides AI literacy training, and pilots responsible AI initiatives for impactful organizations. Their proprietary AI platform, augment.impact, enables the creation of advanced AI analysts and advisors (aia2), helping clients efficiently transform data into actionable insights.

The AI Engineering Fellowship Program

The 6-month fellowship is designed to develop future leaders in AI and data science. Selected fellows will:

  • Rotate through roles including data engineer, data scientist, and Gen AI specialist
  • Contribute to building data pipelines, AI solutions, and visualization tools
  • Participate in code reviews and improve technical processes
  • Drive initiatives to enhance workflows and implement innovative solutions

While full-time employment is not guaranteed, high-performing fellows may be offered permanent positions within Dalberg.

Why Join the Fellowship?

The program offers unmatched professional and technical development:

  • Technical Mastery: Build advanced skills in AI, data engineering, and data science
  • Professional Growth: Understand project responsibilities, explore your strengths, and align with organizational needs
  • Impactful Work: Contribute to AI-driven solutions for global challenges such as pandemics, climate change, and youth unemployment

Who Should Apply

Ideal candidates are curious, resourceful, and self-driven. You should:

  • Have a strong background in AI, Data Science, or related fields
  • Be proficient in Python and other programming languages
  • Thrive in multicultural teams and fast-paced environments
  • Be authorized to work in Kenya

Key Dates

Application Deadline: April 17, 2026
Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, so early submission is encouraged.

Join Dalberg Data Insights

If you’re ready to launch your career in AI and data for social impact, apply for the AI Engineering Fellowship 2026 in Nairobi, Kenya. Transform your skills into solutions that drive global change.

APPLY HERE. 

For more information about this apportunity, click here. 

Discover more fellowship opportunities, click here. 

The post Launch Your Career in AI and Data for Global Impact: Dalberg Data Insights AI Engineering Fellowship 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
